stores.name: 7-Eleven
stores.intro:
7-Eleven runs about 9,500 U.S. convenience stores, many with attached gas pumps. The
pumps code as gas on every card we track. In-store purchases (snacks, drinks, hot food)
typically code as convenience stores rather than as groceries or dining, which means
most 3-5% category bonuses don't apply to non-fuel purchases at 7-Eleven.

stores.name: Ace Hardware
stores.intro:
Ace Hardware is a cooperative of about 5,000 independently-owned hardware stores in the
U.S., smaller and more service-oriented than Home Depot or Lowe's. Ace's own Ace Rewards
loyalty program layers on top of card cashback. Notably, Ace Hardware is one of the
merchants that earns 3% on the Apple Card via Apple Pay, alongside the home-improvement
category match on general-purpose cards.

stores.name: Adidas
stores.intro:
Adidas is the second-largest global athletic apparel brand. Adidas.com codes as
online shopping; in-store charges code as department stores. The adiClub loyalty
program issues spend-tier credits and member-exclusive product drops. Strongest
pairings are an online-shopping-bonus card for adidas.com or flat-rate cashback
for in-store.

stores.name: Advance Auto Parts
stores.intro:
Advance Auto Parts is a U.S. auto parts retailer with about 4,700 domestic
stores plus the Carquest commercial parts network. In-store charges code as
auto parts; advance auto parts.com codes as online shopping. The Speed Perks
loyalty program is free and issues percent-back rewards in store credit. Pair
with flat-rate cashback or an online-shopping-bonus card for digital orders.

stores.name: Airbnb
stores.intro:
Airbnb is the dominant short-term-rental marketplace, with millions of listings across
homes, apartments, cabins, and unique stays in roughly 220 countries. The wrinkle for
credit-card rewards is that Airbnb does not code as "hotels" — it codes as a generic
travel merchant. That means hotel-category bonuses (Marriott co-brand 6x, Amex Gold
hotel rate via Resy collection, Hilton Surpass 12x at hotels) generally do not trigger
on an Airbnb charge. The right cards for Airbnb are broad travel-category earners:
Sapphire Reserve and Preferred at 3x and 2x travel, Venture and Venture X at 2x flat
with no foreign transaction fees, Amex Green at 3x travel, or flat-rate 2%+ cashback
cards. Booking through the Chase or Capital One travel portals can layer additional
multipliers on top, though portal availability for Airbnb is limited.

faq.q: Does Airbnb count as a hotel for credit card rewards?
faq.a: No. Airbnb codes as a general travel merchant, not lodging or hotels. Cards that bonus on "hotels" specifically (most co-brand hotel cards, some category bonuses tied to hotel merchant codes) typically do not earn the elevated rate on Airbnb stays. Cards that bonus on "travel" broadly — Sapphire Reserve/Preferred, Venture/Venture X, Amex Green — do earn at their travel rate on Airbnb.

faq.q: What's the best credit card for Airbnb?
faq.a: For most people: Chase Sapphire Reserve at 3x travel (or Sapphire Preferred at 2x) if you want flexible points, Capital One Venture X at 2x with travel credits and no foreign transaction fees, or a flat 2%+ cashback card like Citi Double Cash if you want simplicity. Hotel co-brand cards generally don't help here.

faq.q: Can I earn hotel elite credits or points on Airbnb stays?
faq.a: No. Airbnb is not part of any hotel loyalty program — stays earn no Bonvoy, Honors, World of Hyatt, IHG One, Wyndham, or Choice points, and don't count toward elite-night requirements. The only rewards on an Airbnb stay come from your credit card.

stores.name: Alaska Airlines
stores.intro:
Alaska Airlines is the West Coast's dominant carrier and runs the Mileage Plan
program, prized for its generous partner award chart and a unique stopover-on-one-way
rule. Alaska tickets code as airfare on every card we track. Alaska's co-branded
Visa offers a Companion Fare each year that's often the single biggest reason
cardholders keep it, plus a free checked bag. General travel cards usually win on
cash-equivalent earn, but the Companion Fare can outweigh that for a once-a-year
long-haul booking.

stores.name: Albertsons
stores.intro:
Albertsons is a national supermarket operator with about 2,250 stores under the
Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Jewel-Osco, Shaw's, Acme, Tom Thumb, and Randalls banners.
All banners code as groceries at the register and ship online through their respective
apps and sites. Albertsons' Just for U coupons stack with card-side rewards at checkout.

stores.name: Aldi
stores.intro:
Aldi is the discount-focused private-label grocer with about 2,400 U.S. stores and a
sharply lower price floor than full-line supermarkets. Average tickets are smaller, so
the absolute dollar value of a 3-6% grocery card is lower per visit, but the rate still
applies. Aldi codes as groceries on every card we track. Note: Aldi historically only
accepted debit and SNAP, but all U.S. stores now accept credit cards.

stores.name: Amazon

also_earns.note: If selected as one of your 2 quarterly retailers (up to $1,500/quarter, then 1.5%)

stores.intro:
Amazon.com is the dominant U.S. online retailer and a category unto itself in many
credit-card rewards programs. Several cards explicitly bonus on Amazon purchases (often
quarterly, often tied to Prime membership), and Amazon.com always codes as online
shopping for cards with that as a generic bonus category. The rule of thumb: an
Amazon-specific bonus almost always beats a generic online-shopping bonus, but caps and
Prime requirements can flip the math for heavy shoppers.

stores.name: AMC Theatres
stores.intro:
AMC Theatres is the largest U.S. movie-theater chain with about 600 domestic
locations. In-theater and online ticket purchases code as entertainment on
every card we track. The free AMC Stubs Insider program and paid AMC Stubs
A-List ($19.95-$24.99/mo for unlimited matinees) deliver the strongest
per-visit savings. Capital One Savor cards earn 4% on entertainment, and
Citi Custom Cash earns 5% if entertainment is your top monthly category.

stores.name: American Airlines
stores.intro:
American Airlines is the world's largest carrier by fleet size and the home of the
AAdvantage loyalty program. Tickets booked at aa.com or at the airport code as
airfare on every card we track. Co-branded AAdvantage cards layer extra miles plus
perks like priority boarding and a free checked bag. General travel cards (Sapphire
Reserve, Venture X) often deliver higher cash-equivalent value if you don't need
AA-specific perks. Tickets booked through OTAs still earn miles and elite credit
if your AAdvantage number is on the reservation.

stores.name: American Eagle
stores.intro:
American Eagle is a U.S. apparel retailer with about 950 stores in its main
brand plus the Aerie intimates spinoff. In-store charges code as department
stores or apparel; ae.com codes as online shopping. The Real Rewards loyalty
program issues spend-based credits redeemable on future purchases. Strongest
pairings are an online-shopping-bonus card for ae.com orders or flat-rate
cashback in-store.

stores.name: Anthropologie
stores.intro:
Anthropologie is a U.S. lifestyle retailer with about 200 stores selling apparel,
home goods, and accessories, owned by URBN (also Urban Outfitters and Free People).
In-store charges code as department stores; anthropologie.com codes as online
shopping. AnthroPerks (free) and the AnthroPerks+ paid tier add free shipping and
birthday rewards. Strongest pairings are an online-shopping-bonus card or
flat-rate cashback for in-store.

stores.name: Apple

also_earns.note: If selected as one of your 2 quarterly retailers (up to $1,500/quarter, then 1.5%)

stores.intro:
Apple's own retail channels — apple.com, the Apple Store app, and ~270 physical Apple
Stores — code as online shopping for most cards on the .com side and as electronics
in-store. Apple purchases through Apple Pay also enable several merchant-specific
bonuses on cards that bonus Apple Pay, but those typically require the purchase to be
made via the Apple Pay button rather than chip-and-PIN. The Apple Card itself earns 3%
on Apple-branded purchases, which is the strongest non-promotional rate you'll find
here.

stores.name: Applebee's
stores.intro:
Applebee's is a U.S. casual-dining chain with about 1,500 domestic locations,
owned by Dine Brands. Charges code as dining on every card we track, so any
dining-bonus card applies (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%).
Applebee's runs frequent prix-fixe deals and a free-appetizer-with-rewards
loyalty program that stack on top of any card's dining bonus.

stores.name: Arby's
stores.intro:
Arby's is a U.S. fast-food chain with about 3,300 domestic locations, focused on
roast beef and deli sandwiches. Charges code as dining on every card we track,
so any dining-bonus card (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%)
applies. The Arby's Rewards app issues per-purchase points redeemable for menu
items, stackable with card bonuses.

stores.name: AutoZone
stores.intro:
AutoZone is the largest U.S. auto parts retailer with about 6,300 domestic
stores. In-store charges typically code as auto parts (some banks classify
as gas-adjacent), and autozone.com codes as online shopping. The AutoZone
Rewards loyalty program is free and issues a $20 reward after five $20+
purchases. Pair with a flat-rate cashback card or, for online orders, an
online-shopping-bonus card.

stores.name: Avis
stores.intro:
Avis is one of the three large U.S. car-rental brands, sister company to Budget under
Avis Budget Group. Rentals code as car rentals on every card we track. Several
premium cards confer Avis President's Club status (Amex Platinum, Capital One Venture
X) which adds a guaranteed upgrade plus free additional driver. Booking via a card's
travel portal earns the portal bonus rate but may forfeit Avis Preferred earnings
and direct-booking promo codes.

stores.name: Bath & Body Works
stores.intro:
Bath & Body Works is a U.S. personal-care chain with about 1,800 domestic stores,
selling soaps, lotions, candles, and home fragrance. In-store charges code as
department stores; bathandbodyworks.com codes as online shopping. The My Bath &
Body Works Rewards program issues a free reward at sign-up plus per-spend points;
a Comenity Bank-issued co-branded credit card layers additional cashback for
heavy shoppers.

stores.name: Best Buy
stores.intro:
Best Buy is the largest U.S. specialty electronics retailer, with about 950 stores and a
strong bestbuy.com presence. In-store and online purchases generally code as electronics
or online shopping rather than as a department store, so the answer here usually comes
down to a flat online-retail card or a flat-rate card. The My Best Buy Visa offers
in-house financing and 5-6% back at Best Buy specifically, but the math only works for
shoppers spending several thousand dollars a year on electronics.

stores.name: Best Western
stores.intro:
Best Western Hotels & Resorts runs about 4,000 properties globally under a member-owned
cooperative structure that's unusual in the major-chain landscape. The portfolio spans
Best Western, Best Western Plus, and Best Western Premier on the core brand, with
Vīb, GLō, Aiden, Sadie, and Executive Residency rounding out boutique and extended-stay
segments, plus the SureStay budget brands. There's no Best Western co-brand credit
card in the U.S. market today, so the right play is a strong general hotel-category
card — Sapphire Reserve, Venture X, Amex Gold, or a flat-rate cashback card if you
prefer simplicity. Best Western Rewards points are earned only on direct bookings at
bestwestern.com or in-app; OTA stays earn nothing in the program.

stores.name: BJ's Wholesale Club
stores.intro:
BJ's Wholesale Club is the East Coast-focused warehouse retailer with about 240 clubs
and a growing online catalog. Unlike Costco and Sam's Club, BJ's accepts manufacturer
coupons in addition to its own discounts, which can stack on top of card rewards. BJ's
accepts all four card networks, and BJ's gas pumps code as wholesale clubs — not as gas
— so a flat 5% gas card will not earn at the pump here.

stores.name: Bloomingdale's
stores.intro:
Bloomingdale's is a luxury-leaning department store with about 30 full-line locations
in the U.S., plus the higher-end Bloomingdale's by Bloomingdale's outlet stores. Most
shoppers are paying full price on apparel, beauty, and home goods, so the cashback rate
on your card matters more here than at a discount retailer.

Bloomingdale's accepts all four major networks (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) and
Apple Pay in stores. There is a co-branded Bloomingdale's American Express that earns
Loyallist points, but most general-purpose cards will pay you better in cashback unless
you spend several thousand dollars a year at Bloomingdale's specifically.

stores.name: Booking.com
stores.intro:
Booking.com is one of the largest global online travel agencies, owned by Booking
Holdings (which also owns Priceline, Kayak, and Agoda). Bookings code as travel
on every card we track. The honest catch: hotel chains typically do NOT credit
Booking.com stays toward elite-night or status counts, and many properties
exclude OTA stays from earning their loyalty points — so a Hilton/Marriott/Hyatt
loyalist almost always books direct. Booking's own Genius rewards tier and
occasional 10% off promos can still beat direct on price for one-off stays.

stores.name: BP
stores.intro:
BP and its Amoco-branded stations cover roughly 7,000 U.S. locations. Pumps code
cleanly as gas on every card we track. BP runs the BPme app and a separate BP
Visa with cash-back at BP/Amoco; in absolute cents most general-purpose 4-5% gas
cards beat it. The bigger swing for heavy BP buyers is the BPme Rewards loyalty
program, which stacks on top of any card's gas bonus.

stores.name: Budget
stores.intro:
Budget is the value-priced sister brand to Avis under Avis Budget Group. Rentals
code as car rentals on every card we track. Pricing is generally a tier below Avis
for the same fleet, and the Avis Preferred program covers Budget rentals too.
General travel cards (Sapphire Preferred, Venture, Venture X) are the standard
pairing — most users book direct rather than via a card's travel portal because
Budget's coupon ecosystem (BCD codes, AAA, Costco) usually beats portal pricing.

stores.name: Buffalo Wild Wings
stores.intro:
Buffalo Wild Wings is a U.S. casual-dining chain with about 1,200 domestic
locations, focused on wings and sports bar atmosphere. Charges code as dining
on every card we track. Any dining-bonus card applies (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire
Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%). Blazin' Rewards in the app issues per-purchase
points redeemable for free items, stackable with card bonuses.

stores.name: Burger King
stores.intro:
Burger King is a U.S. fast-food chain with about 7,000 domestic locations, owned
by Restaurant Brands International. Charges code as dining on every card we track.
Standard dining-bonus cards (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%) are
the obvious pairings. The Royal Perks loyalty program in the app issues "Crowns"
redeemable for free menu items, stackable with any card's bonus rate.

stores.name: Burlington
stores.intro:
Burlington is a U.S. off-price retail chain with about 1,000 stores selling
apparel, home, and baby goods at discount prices. Codes as department stores on
most cards. There's no co-branded Burlington credit card, so the strongest
pairings are department-store-bonus or flat-rate cashback cards. Burlington
doesn't run a high-volume loyalty program.

stores.name: Carnival Cruise Line
stores.intro:
Carnival Cruise Line is the largest U.S. cruise operator by passenger volume,
owned by Carnival Corporation (which also owns Princess, Holland America, and
Cunard). Cruise bookings code as travel on every card we track. Pre-booked
shore excursions, drink packages, and gratuities purchased through the cruise
line generally code as travel as well. Onboard charges (folio purchases) often
code as travel but sometimes as cruise-specific — worth confirming on a small
charge before relying on a 3x travel multiplier.

stores.name: Chevron
stores.intro:
Chevron and Texaco-branded stations cover about 8,000 U.S. locations primarily in the
West and South. Pumps code cleanly as gas on every card we track. The Chevron Visa
offers extra cents-per-gallon discounts at Chevron and Texaco stations, but most
general-purpose gas-bonus cards beat it on absolute cashback unless you're a heavy
Chevron-only buyer.

stores.name: Chewy
stores.intro:
Chewy is the largest dedicated U.S. online pet retailer, with autoship being the
dominant purchase pattern for repeat customers. All Chewy purchases code as online
shopping on every card we track. Pet-supply spend doesn't fall into a dedicated card
category on most general-purpose cards, so the right answer here is whichever U.S.
online retail card you carry — Blue Cash Everyday, Hilton Surpass, or a flat 2% card
depending on cap considerations.

stores.name: Chick-fil-A
stores.intro:
Chick-fil-A is one of the highest-grossing U.S. fast-food chains by per-store revenue,
with about 3,000 locations and a closed-loop app/loyalty program. All Chick-fil-A
purchases code as dining/restaurants on every card we track. App reloads of Chick-fil-A
One credits also trigger the dining category, which is a clean way to capture the bonus.

stores.name: Chili's
stores.intro:
Chili's is a U.S. casual-dining chain with about 1,200 domestic locations, owned
by Brinker International. Charges code as dining on every card we track, so
any dining-bonus card applies (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%).
My Chili's Rewards offers a free chips-and-salsa or non-alcoholic drink with
each visit and frequent BOGO entree promotions, stackable with card bonuses.

stores.name: Chipotle
stores.intro:
Chipotle is one of the largest U.S. fast-casual chains at about 3,500 locations, with a
well-developed digital ordering app and Chipotle Rewards loyalty program. All Chipotle
purchases code as dining/restaurants on every card we track, so 3-5% dining-bonus cards
earn at the same rate whether you order in-store, on the app, or for delivery direct
from Chipotle.

stores.name: Choice Hotels
stores.intro:
Choice Hotels operates roughly 7,500 properties worldwide, dominated by mid-scale and
economy brands — Comfort Inn, Quality Inn, Sleep Inn, Clarion — with the Cambria and
Ascend Hotel Collection covering its upscale push. The 2022 acquisition of Radisson
Americas folded Country Inn & Suites and Park Plaza into Choice Privileges, broadening
the footprint considerably. Award nights start as low as 6,000 points, and the program
is one of the better economy-tier values on a cents-per-point basis. The Wells Fargo
Choice Privileges co-brand cards earn elevated points on Choice stays and gas, with
the Select tier adding automatic Platinum elite status and an annual bonus night.
Direct bookings earn points; OTA stays do not.

stores.name: Circle K
stores.intro:
Circle K is one of the largest U.S. convenience-store chains, with about 7,000
domestic locations operating both branded fuel and convenience retail. Fuel
purchases code as gas on every card we track; in-store purchases code as
convenience or grocery depending on the bank. The Inner Circle membership
($1.99/mo) layers on per-gallon discounts and is usually a stronger rebate per
visit than any Circle K-specific credit card.

stores.name: Costco

also_earns.note: If selected as one of your 2 quarterly retailers (up to $1,500/quarter, then 1.5%)

stores.intro:
Costco Wholesale runs about 600 warehouses worldwide and a sizable online catalog at
costco.com. The pricing model revolves around member-only bulk buying, so card choice
matters most for people running weekly grocery and gas trips through the warehouse. Two
quirks worth knowing: Costco accepts only Visa cards in-warehouse (no Mastercard, Amex,
or Discover), and Costco gas pumps code as wholesale clubs — not gas — so a 5% gas card
will not trigger at the pump here.

stores.name: Crate & Barrel
stores.intro:
Crate & Barrel is a U.S. home furnishings retailer with about 100 stores plus
the CB2 modern-design sister brand. In-store charges code as furniture; online
orders code as online shopping. The Crate & Barrel Credit Card and Reward
Mastercard issue percent-back in store credit on Crate & Barrel-family purchases;
the Reward Mastercard adds a flat rate on everyday spend. Pair otherwise with
an online-shopping-bonus card.

stores.name: CVS
stores.intro:
CVS is the largest U.S. pharmacy chain by store count (about 9,000 locations) and a
major convenience-goods retailer alongside the prescription business. CVS codes as a
drugstore on every card we track, so any 3-5% drugstore-category card will earn here.
CVS's own ExtraCare loyalty program runs in parallel and stacks with card cashback.

stores.name: Delta Air Lines
stores.intro:
Delta Air Lines runs one of the strongest U.S. domestic networks and the SkyMiles
loyalty program. Delta tickets code as airfare across all major cards. Co-branded
SkyMiles Amex cards add extra miles per dollar on Delta plus perks like a free
checked bag, Sky Club access (Reserve only), and Medallion-status boost spend.
General travel cards usually beat them on raw cash-equivalent value, but the
Reserve's lounge access and MQD-waiver path can swing the math for frequent flyers.
OTA bookings still earn miles when your SkyMiles number is attached.

stores.name: Dick's Sporting Goods
stores.intro:
Dick's Sporting Goods is the largest U.S. sporting goods retailer with about
720 namesake stores plus Public Lands, Field & Stream, Going Going Gone, and
Golf Galaxy banners. In-store charges code as department stores; online orders
code as online shopping. The free ScoreCard loyalty program issues 1 point per
$1 plus periodic 2-3x bonus events redeemable for store credit, stackable with
card bonuses. There's a Dick's-branded Synchrony credit card with 8% in-store
cashback for cardholders.

stores.name: Disney+
stores.intro:
Disney+ is The Walt Disney Company's streaming service, with Disney/Pixar/Marvel/
Star Wars/National Geographic content. Monthly subscriptions code as streaming on
every card we track. Streaming-bonus cards (Blue Cash Preferred 6%, U.S. Bank
Cash+ 5% on selected categories) are the strongest pairings. Disney+ is also
bundled with Hulu and ESPN+ as the Disney Bundle, often the cheapest path to
all three services.

stores.name: Dollar General
stores.intro:
Dollar General is a U.S. discount retail chain with about 20,000 domestic stores,
often the only general-merchandise option in rural ZIP codes. Codes as discount
stores or department stores depending on the card. The DG Rewards loyalty program
in the app issues digital coupons and frequent buyer rewards on top of any
card's bonus rate.

stores.name: Dollar Tree
stores.intro:
Dollar Tree is a U.S. discount retail chain with about 8,000 domestic stores,
selling household goods, snacks, and party supplies at fixed price points.
Codes as discount stores or department stores depending on the card; some banks
classify it as grocery in mixed-merchandise transactions. Average ticket sizes
are very small, so the absolute earn from any bonus card is modest. A flat-rate
2% cashback card is usually the cleanest pairing.

stores.name: Domino's
stores.intro:
Domino's is the largest U.S. pizza chain at about 6,800 locations and a heavy digital
ordering footprint. All Domino's transactions — phone, app, and dominos.com — code as
dining on every card we track, regardless of whether the food is delivered or picked up.
The Domino's Rewards program (free pizza after a points threshold) stacks on top of card
cashback.

stores.name: DoorDash
stores.intro:
DoorDash is the largest U.S. food-delivery service by market share, covering both
restaurant delivery and (via DashMart and grocery partners) household goods. Restaurant
orders code as dining/restaurants on every card we track, and several cards specifically
call out DoorDash for elevated dining bonuses or DashPass perks. DashMart and
grocery-partner orders typically code as the underlying merchant rather than as dining.

stores.name: Dunkin'
stores.intro:
Dunkin' is a U.S. coffee-and-donut chain with about 9,000 domestic locations.
Charges code as dining on every card we track, so any restaurant-bonus card
applies — Amex Gold's 4x dining (capped), Capital One SavorOne's 3%, Sapphire
Preferred's 3x. Heavy regulars also use the free DD Perks/Dunkin' Rewards app
for free drinks and bonus-point promotions, which stack on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: eBay
stores.intro:
eBay is the largest U.S. online auction and resale marketplace and codes as online
shopping on essentially every credit card we track. Unlike Amazon, eBay rarely has a
card-specific bonus carve-out, so the right card here is the strongest generic
online-shopping earner you carry. Watch for cards with a U.S. online retail cap — eBay
payments via PayPal still count as eBay for category coding, but third-party processor
purchases on eBay's classifieds side may not.

stores.name: Enterprise
stores.intro:
Enterprise is the largest U.S. car-rental company by fleet, owner of National and
Alamo. Rentals code as car rentals on every card we track. Status matches into
Enterprise Plus are less common than at Hertz/Avis, so most cardholders pair
Enterprise with a general travel card for the bonus on car rentals plus secondary
rental insurance. Booking through a card's travel portal earns the portal multiplier
but may not credit Enterprise Plus points.

stores.name: Etsy
stores.intro:
Etsy is the marketplace for handmade, vintage, and craft goods sold by independent
makers, with checkout handled by Etsy itself. Purchases code as online shopping on every
general-purpose card we track, which means Etsy is a good fit for any card with a flat
U.S. online retail bonus (Blue Cash Everyday, Hilton Surpass, etc.). Etsy doesn't have a
co-branded card and rarely shows up in card-specific bonus lists.

stores.name: Expedia
stores.intro:
Expedia is one of the largest online travel agencies, owned by Expedia Group
(which also owns Hotels.com, Vrbo, and Travelocity). Bookings code as travel on
every card we track. The honest catch: hotel and airline programs typically do
NOT credit Expedia bookings toward elite-night or elite-qualifying-mile counts,
and many properties exclude OTA stays from earning their own loyalty points —
so booking direct usually wins for status-conscious travelers. The Expedia
Rewards Cards from Citi layer extra OneKeyCash on Expedia-family bookings.

stores.name: ExxonMobil
stores.intro:
ExxonMobil operates Exxon and Mobil-branded stations at about 11,500 U.S. locations,
plus the ExxonMobil Rewards+ app for in-station discounts. ExxonMobil pumps code as gas
on every card we track, and one merchant-specific note: ExxonMobil purchases earn 3% on
the Apple Card when paid via Apple Pay.

stores.name: Five Guys
stores.intro:
Five Guys is a U.S. fast-casual burger chain with about 1,700 domestic locations,
known for hand-formed patties and unlimited toppings. Charges code as dining on
every card we track, so any dining-bonus card applies (Amex Gold 4x within cap,
Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%). Average ticket sizes are higher than typical
fast-food, which makes the dining multiplier more meaningful per visit.

stores.name: Food Lion
stores.intro:
Food Lion is a Southeastern U.S. grocery chain with about 1,100 stores, owned by
Ahold Delhaize. Codes cleanly as groceries on every card we track. Strongest
pairings are Blue Cash Preferred (6% on U.S. supermarkets), the Capital One SavorOne
(3% groceries), and U.S. Bank Shopper Cash Rewards if Food Lion is one of the two
selected retailers. The MVP loyalty program runs separate digital coupons that
stack on top of any card's bonus rate.

stores.name: Frontier Airlines
stores.intro:
Frontier Airlines is a U.S. ultra-low-cost carrier with a heavy hub presence in
Denver. Base fares are very low and almost everything else (bags, seats, carry-on)
is à la carte. Tickets code as airfare on every card we track. Frontier offers
a co-branded Mastercard but no widely-recommended option, so general travel cards
are the standard pairing — and the small fares mean trip-protection benefits
rarely move the needle.

stores.name: GameStop
stores.intro:
GameStop is the largest U.S. video-game specialty retailer with about 2,900
domestic stores. In-store charges typically code as electronics or hobby;
gamestop.com codes as online shopping. The PowerUp Rewards program is free
(Pro tier is paid) and issues per-purchase points plus a Game Informer
subscription on the Pro tier. There's no widely-recommended GameStop-specific
credit card; pair with an online-shopping-bonus card or flat-rate cashback.

stores.name: Gap
stores.intro:
Gap is a U.S. apparel retailer with about 500 domestic stores plus a sister
brand portfolio (Old Navy, Banana Republic, Athleta) under Gap Inc. In-store
charges code as department stores or apparel; gap.com codes as online shopping.
The Gap Good Rewards co-branded Mastercard offers tiered cashback at Gap-family
brands. For non-cardholders, an online-shopping-bonus card or U.S. Bank Shopper
Cash Rewards (if Gap is selected) is the strongest pairing.

stores.name: Giant Food
stores.intro:
Giant Food is the Mid-Atlantic flagship of Ahold Delhaize's U.S. grocery group, with
about 165 stores across DC, Maryland, Virginia, and Delaware. Codes cleanly as
groceries on every card we track. Best pairings are Blue Cash Preferred (6%),
Capital One SavorOne (3%), and U.S. Bank Shopper Cash Rewards (6% if selected).
The Giant Flexible Rewards program issues per-gallon fuel savings at participating
Shell and Giant stations that stack on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: H-E-B
stores.intro:
H-E-B is the dominant Texas supermarket chain, with about 425 stores under H-E-B,
Central Market, and Joe V's banners. The chain is privately held and well-regarded for
store-brand products and Texas-only specialty items. H-E-B codes as groceries on every
card we track and supports curbside pickup and delivery on most product lines.

stores.name: H&M
stores.intro:
H&M is a Swedish-headquartered fast-fashion chain with about 540 U.S. stores.
In-store charges code as department stores or apparel; hm.com codes as online
shopping. The H&M Member program is free and issues birthday gifts plus tiered
perks; H&M's co-branded U.S. credit card layers an additional discount on top
of the loyalty program. For non-cardholders, an online-shopping-bonus card is
the strongest pairing.

stores.name: Hannaford
stores.intro:
Hannaford is a New England grocery chain with about 185 stores, owned by Ahold
Delhaize. Codes cleanly as groceries on every card we track. Strongest pairings
are Blue Cash Preferred (6%), Capital One SavorOne (3% groceries with no cap),
and U.S. Bank Shopper Cash Rewards (6% on selected retailers). Hannaford runs
the My Hannaford Rewards program with personalized digital coupons on top of
any card's earn rate.

stores.name: Harris Teeter
stores.intro:
Harris Teeter is a Southeast U.S. grocery chain with about 250 stores, owned by
Kroger. Codes cleanly as groceries on every card we track. Best pairings are
Blue Cash Preferred (6%), Capital One SavorOne (3%), and U.S. Bank Shopper Cash
Rewards (6% if Harris Teeter is selected). VIC card (loyalty) discounts and
per-gallon fuel savings at Harris Teeter and Kroger fuel stations stack on top
of card bonuses.

stores.name: Hawaiian Airlines
stores.intro:
Hawaiian Airlines is the dominant carrier between the U.S. mainland and Hawaii, plus
inter-island routes. Tickets code as airfare on every card we track. The co-branded
Hawaiian Airlines World Elite Mastercard offers a discount on round-trip companion
tickets to Hawaii each year and bonus miles on the airline. With Alaska Airlines'
acquisition closed, Hawaiian's loyalty program is integrating with Alaska's Mileage
Plan, so long-term value will increasingly route through Alaska's award chart.

stores.name: Hertz
stores.intro:
Hertz is one of the three large U.S. car-rental brands and runs the Gold Plus Rewards
program. Rentals booked direct at hertz.com code as car rentals on every card we
track. Several premium cards offer status matches with Hertz President's Circle
(Capital One Venture X, Amex Platinum), which adds a class upgrade and free
additional driver. Bookings made through a card's travel portal earn the portal
bonus rate but typically lose any direct-booking discount and may not always credit
to your Gold Plus Rewards account.

stores.name: Hilton
stores.intro:
Hilton Honors covers roughly 8,000 properties worldwide across 22 brands, from budget
Hampton Inn and Tru up through Waldorf Astoria and Conrad luxury. Booking direct at
hilton.com or in-app earns the full 10x base points plus any co-brand multiplier, while
third-party bookings (Expedia, Booking.com) earn nothing in the program and don't count
toward elite status. The Hilton co-brand cards lean heavily on perks — free weekend
nights, automatic Gold or Diamond status, statement credits — rather than headline
earn rates, so the value calculation depends on whether you'll actually use the
certificate and the status. Hilton points typically value around 0.5 cents each.

stores.name: Hobby Lobby
stores.intro:
Hobby Lobby is a U.S. arts-and-crafts retailer with about 1,000 stores, selling
fabric, home decor, art supplies, and seasonal goods. In-store charges code as
department stores or specialty retail; hobbylobby.com codes as online shopping.
Hobby Lobby doesn't run a loyalty program but consistently posts a 40%-off-one-
item coupon that's the main rewards lever per visit. Pair with a flat-rate
cashback card or department-store-bonus card.

stores.name: Home Depot
stores.intro:
The Home Depot is the largest U.S. home-improvement retailer, with about 2,000 stores
and a heavily-used Pro Xtra business loyalty program. The right card here depends a lot
on whether you're a homeowner doing one project a year or a contractor running steady
volume — the gap between the best home-improvement category card and a generic 2% card
is meaningful at contractor spend levels. Home Depot codes as home improvement at the
register and on homedepot.com.

stores.name: HomeGoods
stores.intro:
HomeGoods is the home-furnishings off-price chain owned by TJX Companies, with
about 900 U.S. stores selling discount furniture, decor, and bath. Codes as
department stores on most cards. The TJX Rewards Mastercard offers 5% back at
HomeGoods and the rest of the TJX family. For everyone else, a department-store
category card (Wells Fargo Active Cash, Citi Custom Cash if it's your top
category) or flat-rate cashback card is the standard pairing.

stores.name: Hotels.com
stores.intro:
Hotels.com is one of the largest global online travel agencies, owned by Expedia
Group. Bookings code as travel on every card we track. The honest catch: hotel
chains typically do NOT credit Hotels.com stays toward elite-night or status
counts, and many properties exclude OTA stays from earning their loyalty points
— so a Hilton/Marriott/Hyatt loyalist almost always books direct. Hotels.com's
own One Key Rewards (formerly Hotels.com Rewards) is now unified with Expedia's
OneKeyCash and stacks across the Expedia family.

stores.name: Hulu
stores.intro:
Hulu is a U.S. streaming-video service offering on-demand library content and a
live-TV bundle, owned by Disney. Monthly subscriptions code as streaming on
every card we track. Streaming-bonus cards (Blue Cash Preferred 6%, U.S. Bank
Cash+ 5% on selected categories) are the strongest pairings. Hulu (with-ads)
is also bundled with Disney+ and ESPN+ as the Disney Bundle for the lowest
per-service cost.

stores.name: Hyatt
stores.intro:
World of Hyatt is the smallest of the major U.S. hotel programs by footprint — about
1,400 properties — but consistently rates as the most rewarding on a points-per-dollar
basis, with award nights starting at 3,500 points at Category 1 properties. Hyatt
points are widely valued at 1.7 to 2.0 cents each, the highest in the hotel space, so
even modest co-brand earn rates produce strong cashback-equivalent value. Direct
bookings at hyatt.com earn points and elite credit; OTA stays earn neither. The Chase
World of Hyatt cards are also one of the few flexible-points pipelines into Hyatt,
since Chase Ultimate Rewards transfer to Hyatt 1:1 — making Sapphire Preferred and
Reserve indirect Hyatt earners as well.

stores.name: IHG
stores.intro:
IHG Hotels & Resorts spans about 6,000 properties across 19 brands, anchored by Holiday
Inn and Holiday Inn Express on the mass-market end and InterContinental, Regent, and
Six Senses on the luxury end. The Chase IHG One Rewards Premier card is one of the
highest-leverage hotel co-brands available — its annual free-night certificate (good
at any property up to 40,000 points) often pays back the annual fee on a single use,
and the fourth-night-free benefit on award stays effectively cuts redemption costs by
25%. Direct bookings at ihg.com or in-app earn points and elite-night credits; OTA
stays generally earn nothing. Points value lands around 0.5 cents but redemptions can
exceed that on aspirational properties.

stores.name: IKEA
stores.intro:
IKEA is the largest global home-furnishings retailer with about 50 U.S. warehouse
stores plus a planning-studio network. In-store charges typically code as
furniture or home improvement; ikea.com codes as online shopping. The IKEA
Family loyalty program is free and issues member-only pricing plus a free
coffee/tea on store visits. The IKEA Visa Credit Card offers 5% back at IKEA
on purchases — typically the strongest rate for heavy buyers.

stores.name: Instacart
stores.intro:
Instacart is the largest U.S. grocery delivery and pickup marketplace, partnering
with Costco, Wegmans, ALDI, Publix, and many regional chains. Orders placed through
the Instacart app or instacart.com generally code as groceries on most cards, but
some banks classify the charge as the underlying merchant — worth confirming on
a small order before relying on a 6% grocery card. Several premium cards (Sapphire
Reserve, Venture X) include Instacart+ memberships and statement credits.

stores.name: J.Crew
stores.intro:
J.Crew is a U.S. apparel retailer with about 130 stores plus a strong online
presence. In-store codes as department stores; jcrew.com codes as online
shopping. The J.Crew Rewards loyalty program issues credits at spend tiers
separate from any card bonus. Strongest pairings are an online-shopping-bonus
card or flat-rate cashback for in-store purchases.

stores.name: JCPenney
stores.intro:
JCPenney is a mid-tier department store with about 660 stores and a smaller online
catalog at jcpenney.com. Like other mid-tier department chains, the bulk of purchases
happen at promotional discount, so card rewards stack on already-reduced prices.
JCPenney codes as a department store in-store and online shopping on the website, so
cards bonusing on either category will trigger here.

stores.name: JetBlue
stores.intro:
JetBlue runs an East Coast and Caribbean network with TrueBlue as its loyalty program.
TrueBlue points are revenue-based — about 1.3 cents each — so they're easy to value
but rarely produce outsized award redemptions. JetBlue tickets code as airfare on
every card we track. The co-branded Plus and Premier cards bundle a 5,000-point
anniversary bonus, free checked bag, and Mosaic-status accelerators that can swing
the math for regulars; the Premier adds a Mint upgrade benefit on transcons.

stores.name: KFC
stores.intro:
KFC is a U.S. fried-chicken fast-food chain with about 4,000 domestic locations,
owned by Yum! Brands. Charges code as dining on every card we track, so any
dining-bonus card applies. Heavy regulars use the KFC Rewards loyalty program
for tiered point earning and free menu items, which stacks on top of any card's
bonus rate.

stores.name: Kohl's
stores.intro:
Kohl's is a mid-tier department store with about 1,150 stores and an aggressive Kohl's
Cash rewards program. Most regular shoppers stack manufacturer sales, Kohl's coupons,
and Kohl's Cash, so card cashback is the smallest of several layers. The Kohl's Card
(issued by Capital One) gives in-house discounts but no MasterCard/Visa-network rewards.
For most shoppers, a department-store-bonus general-purpose card is the right answer.

stores.name: Kroger
stores.intro:
Kroger is the largest dedicated U.S. supermarket operator, running about 2,700 stores
under Kroger and a stack of regional banners (Ralphs, Fred Meyer, Smith's, King Soopers,
Harris Teeter, Fry's, and others). All code as groceries at the register and most do
online shopping via the Kroger app/site. Any standard supermarket-bonus card earns here.
Kroger's own fuel-points program layers on top of card cashback.

stores.name: Lowe's
stores.intro:
Lowe's is the second-largest U.S. home-improvement retailer at about 1,750 stores, with
a long-standing rivalry with Home Depot for both consumer and pro spend. Pricing is
similar between the two, so the right card here is whichever home-improvement-bonus
general-purpose card you carry, plus the Lowe's Advantage credit line if you're
financing a large project. Lowe's codes as home improvement in-store and on lowes.com.

stores.name: Lululemon
stores.intro:
Lululemon is the dominant U.S. athletic-apparel brand with about 700 stores and a heavy
lululemon.com presence. Like other apparel retailers, Lululemon doesn't fall into a card
bonus category in-store — the right answer there is a strong flat-rate cashback card.
Online purchases at lululemon.com code as online shopping, which opens up a different
set of category bonuses.

stores.name: Lyft
stores.intro:
Lyft is the second-largest U.S. rideshare service after Uber. Rides code under
the transit category on most cards. The Chase Sapphire Reserve historically pays
10x on Lyft rides through 2027, and Lyft Pink (Lyft's premium membership) is
comped with several Chase cards. Sapphire Preferred, Venture X, and SavorOne all
earn elevated transit/rideshare rates without the Lyft-specific multipliers.

stores.name: Macy's
stores.intro:
Macy's is the largest U.S. mid-tier department store, with about 500 full-line stores
plus Backstage outlets and a heavy promotional calendar. Most shoppers buy at heavy
discount, so the cashback rate stacks on already-reduced prices. Macy's codes as a
department store at the register and as online shopping on macys.com, which means cards
bonusing on either category can earn here. The Macy's American Express has its own
Loyallist points layer for shoppers doing several thousand a year at Macy's
specifically.

stores.name: Marathon
stores.intro:
Marathon-branded stations cover about 7,000 U.S. locations primarily across the
Midwest and Southeast. Pumps code cleanly as gas on every card we track. The
MakeItCount Rewards program offers cents-per-gallon savings that stack with
whatever card you swipe; absolute cashback usually wins with a 4-5% general gas
card rather than a Marathon-only co-brand.

stores.name: Marriott
stores.intro:
Marriott Bonvoy is the world's largest hotel loyalty program, covering more than 30
brands from budget Fairfield Inn through luxury Ritz-Carlton, St. Regis, and W Hotels.
Direct stays at marriott.com or any Bonvoy property earn the most points and unlock
elite-night credits, but the math gets nuanced: co-branded Marriott cards layer extra
Bonvoy points on top of base earnings, while general hotel-category cards (Sapphire
Reserve, Venture X) often yield higher cashback-equivalent value if you don't care
about Bonvoy status. Stays booked through OTAs like Expedia or Hotels.com forfeit
points and elite credit, so the in-program booking is almost always the right call.

stores.name: Marshalls
stores.intro:
Marshalls is an off-price retail chain owned by TJX Companies, with about 1,200
U.S. stores selling apparel, home, and accessories at discount prices. Codes as
department stores on most cards. The TJX Rewards Mastercard offers 5% back at
Marshalls, TJ Maxx, HomeGoods, Sierra, and Homesense — typically the best rate
for heavy TJX shoppers. Otherwise pair with a department-store-bonus card or a
flat-rate cashback card.

stores.name: Max
stores.intro:
Max is Warner Bros. Discovery's streaming service, the rebranded successor to
HBO Max. Monthly subscriptions code as streaming on every card we track.
Streaming-bonus cards (Blue Cash Preferred 6%, U.S. Bank Cash+ 5% on selected
categories) are the strongest pairings. Max also bundles with Hulu/Disney+ in
some seasonal Disney Bundle promotions.

stores.name: McDonald's
stores.intro:
McDonald's is the largest U.S. quick-service restaurant chain at about 13,500 locations.
All purchases — in-store, drive-thru, and via the McDonald's app — code as
dining/restaurants on every card we track, so any 3-5% dining-category card earns here.
The McDonald's app's MyMcDonald's Rewards program stacks on top of card cashback.

stores.name: Meijer
stores.intro:
Meijer is a Midwest superstore chain with about 240 locations across Michigan,
Ohio, Indiana, Illinois, Kentucky, and Wisconsin — selling groceries, household,
and general merchandise under one roof. Most cards code Meijer as groceries (it's
one of the few "supercenter" chains that does), so a 6% Blue Cash Preferred or
3% SavorOne earns the bonus on Meijer's full basket including non-grocery items.
mPerks runs separate digital coupons that stack on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Menards
stores.intro:
Menards is a Midwest-focused home-improvement chain with about 350 stores, well-known
for its 11% mail-in rebate promotions that effectively act as in-house cashback on top
of card rewards. The math here is interesting: an 11% Menards rebate stacked with a 2%
flat-rate cashback card often beats most home-improvement category cards on net. Menards
codes as home improvement at the register and on menards.com.

stores.name: Michaels
stores.intro:
Michaels is the largest U.S. arts-and-crafts retailer with about 1,300 stores,
selling beads, yarn, frames, and seasonal decor. In-store charges code as
department stores or specialty retail; michaels.com codes as online shopping.
The Michaels Rewards program is free and issues per-spend points redeemable
for in-store credit. Pair with a flat-rate cashback or department-store-bonus
card; there's no widely-recommended Michaels-specific credit card.

stores.name: National Car Rental
stores.intro:
National is the upmarket Enterprise sister brand, beloved by frequent business
travelers for its Emerald Aisle "pick any car" benefit. Rentals code as car rentals
on every card we track. Several premium cards confer Emerald Club Executive (Amex
Platinum, Capital One Venture X) which adds Executive Aisle access — a wider
selection of higher-tier vehicles. National rarely publishes deep promo codes, so
paying with a 3x-on-travel card often beats hunting for portal discounts.

stores.name: Neiman Marcus
stores.intro:
Neiman Marcus is a luxury department store with about 35 full-line stores and a
significant online presence. Average tickets here are among the highest in U.S.
department retail, which shifts the math toward cards that pay a high department-store
category rate without a tight spend cap. NM's own InCircle loyalty program runs
alongside any card cashback you earn at checkout.

stores.name: Netflix
stores.intro:
Netflix is the largest U.S. streaming-video service. Monthly subscriptions code
cleanly as streaming on every card we track. Several mid-tier rewards cards earn
elevated rates on streaming (Blue Cash Preferred 6% on select streaming including
Netflix, U.S. Bank Cash+ 5% if streaming is a chosen category). Card-issued
Netflix credits are rare; the simplest play is pairing the subscription with
whichever streaming-bonus card you already carry.

stores.name: Nike
stores.intro:
Nike is the world's largest athletic apparel and footwear brand, with about 270
U.S. stores plus a strong digital presence. Nike.com and the Nike app code as
online shopping; in-store charges code as department stores. Apple Card pays
3% on Nike via Apple Pay (in-store and online), one of the few merchant-specific
3% Apple Pay bonuses. Otherwise pair with an online-shopping-bonus card for
digital orders and flat-rate cashback for in-store.

stores.name: Nordstrom
stores.intro:
Nordstrom is a higher-end department store with about 100 full-line stores and a much
larger Nordstrom Rack outlet network. Pricing skews full-retail at the flagship
locations and steeply discounted at Rack, so the right card depends partly on which
channel you're shopping. Both code as department stores at the register and online
shopping on nordstrom.com / nordstromrack.com. Nordstrom's own Nordy Club rewards layer
in on top of card cashback.

stores.name: O'Reilly Auto Parts
stores.intro:
O'Reilly Auto Parts is the second-largest U.S. auto parts retailer with about
6,200 domestic stores. In-store charges typically code as auto parts; online
orders code as online shopping. The O'Reilly O'Rewards loyalty program is free
and issues per-purchase points redeemable for store credit. Pair with a
flat-rate cashback card or an online-shopping-bonus card for orders shipped
from oreillyauto.com.

stores.name: Office Depot
stores.intro:
Office Depot (which absorbed OfficeMax) is the second-largest U.S. office-supply
retailer with about 870 stores. In-store and online charges code as office
supplies on every card we track. Chase Ink Business Cash earns 5% back at
Office Depot (capped within the office-supply + telecom $25K/yr bucket) —
typically the strongest pairing for small-business cardholders. The Office
Depot Rewards program adds tier-based points redeemable for store credit.

stores.name: Old Navy
stores.intro:
Old Navy is Gap, Inc.'s value-priced apparel banner with about 1,200 U.S. stores and a
strong online catalog. Apparel doesn't have a dedicated reward category on most
general-purpose credit cards, so card choice usually comes down to whether you're
shopping online (where online-shopping bonuses apply) or in-store (where you're
typically falling back to a strong flat-rate cashback card).

stores.name: Olive Garden
stores.intro:
Olive Garden is a U.S. casual-dining chain with about 900 domestic locations,
owned by Darden Restaurants. Charges code as dining on every card we track, so
Amex Gold 4x (capped), Sapphire Preferred 3x, and SavorOne 3% all earn the
dining bonus. Eligible Darden gift cards purchased through the My Olive Garden
Rewards or Darden portals occasionally code as gift cards rather than dining,
worth checking before bulk-buying.

stores.name: Outback Steakhouse
stores.intro:
Outback Steakhouse is a U.S. casual-dining steakhouse chain with about 700
domestic locations, owned by Bloomin' Brands (also Carrabba's, Bonefish Grill).
Charges code as dining on every card we track. Any dining-bonus card applies
(Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%). The Dine Rewards program
spans Bloomin' Brands sister concepts and stacks on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Panera Bread
stores.intro:
Panera Bread is a fast-casual bakery-cafe with about 2,100 U.S. locations. Charges
code as dining on every card we track. The Unlimited Sip Club ($14.99/mo) provides
unlimited drinks and is occasionally credited by the Capital One SavorOne or Amex
Gold's flexible dining benefits. Standard high-bonus dining cards (Amex Gold 4x,
Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%) are the obvious pairings.

stores.name: Papa John's
stores.intro:
Papa John's is a U.S. pizza-delivery chain with about 3,300 domestic locations.
Charges code as dining on every card we track. Any dining-bonus card (Amex Gold
4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%) applies. The Papa Rewards loyalty
program in the app issues recurring points-for-pizza promotions that stack on
top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Peet's Coffee
stores.intro:
Peet's Coffee is a specialty coffee roaster and cafe chain with about 200 U.S.
cafes, primarily in California. In-cafe charges code as dining on every card we
track. Bagged-bean orders shipped from peets.com sometimes code as online shopping
rather than dining — worth checking before relying on a 4x dining card. Heavy
regulars stack the Peetnik Rewards loyalty program on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Petco
stores.intro:
Petco is one of the two large U.S. pet specialty retailers (alongside PetSmart),
with about 1,500 stores plus in-store grooming and veterinary services. In-store
charges code as pet store / specialty retail on most cards (some classify as
groceries for food-only baskets); petco.com codes as online shopping. The Vital
Care Premier membership ($24.99/mo) credits back monthly spend at Petco. Pair
with a flat-rate cashback or online-shopping-bonus card for petco.com orders.

stores.name: PetSmart
stores.intro:
PetSmart is the largest U.S. pet specialty retailer by store count at about 1,650
locations, with a sizable petsmart.com online catalog. Pet-supply purchases don't have a
dedicated category on most credit cards, so in-store purchases generally fall back to a
flat-rate cashback card. Online purchases at petsmart.com code as online shopping. The
Treats Rewards loyalty program stacks on top of card cashback.

stores.name: Pizza Hut
stores.intro:
Pizza Hut is a U.S. pizza chain with about 6,500 domestic locations, owned by
Yum! Brands. Charges code as dining on every card we track. Any dining-bonus card
applies. The Hut Rewards loyalty program in the app issues per-dollar points
redeemable for free items, stackable with card bonuses.

stores.name: Popeyes
stores.intro:
Popeyes is a U.S. Louisiana-style fried chicken chain with about 3,000 domestic
locations, owned by Restaurant Brands International. Charges code as dining on
every card we track. Any dining-bonus card (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x,
SavorOne 3%) applies. The Popeyes Rewards app issues per-purchase points
redeemable for free items, stackable with card bonuses.

stores.name: Pottery Barn
stores.intro:
Pottery Barn is a U.S. home furnishings retailer with about 200 stores, owned by
Williams-Sonoma Inc. (also West Elm, Pottery Barn Kids). Stores code as
furniture/home improvement; potterybarn.com codes as online shopping. The Key
Rewards loyalty program is free, spans all Williams-Sonoma sister brands, and
earns 2% in Key Rewards points; pairing it with the Key Rewards co-branded
Mastercard adds an additional 5% on Williams-Sonoma family purchases.

stores.name: Publix
stores.intro:
Publix is the largest employee-owned U.S. supermarket chain, with about 1,400 stores
concentrated in the Southeast. Pricing skews higher than at mass-market grocers like
Walmart Neighborhood Market or Aldi, which makes a strong grocery-category card more
valuable per visit. Publix codes cleanly as groceries on every card we track.

stores.name: QuikTrip
stores.intro:
QuikTrip is a South-and-Midwest gas-and-convenience chain with about 1,000 stores,
consistently ranked among the highest customer-satisfaction convenience operators
in the U.S. Fuel codes as gas on every card we track; in-store purchases typically
code as convenience. The QT Rewards app offers per-gallon savings; pair it with a
4-5% general gas card for the cleanest stack.

stores.name: REI
stores.intro:
REI is a member-owned outdoor co-op with about 180 U.S. stores, selling apparel,
camping, climbing, and cycling gear. REI codes under its own merchant category
on several cards (notably the Co-op Mastercard, which earns 5% back at REI for
Co-op Members) and as online shopping for rei.com purchases otherwise. The $30
one-time Co-op membership earns an annual member dividend (~10% of purchases),
plus member-only sales — typically the most valuable lever for heavy REI buyers
beyond any card bonus.

stores.name: Rite Aid
stores.intro:
Rite Aid is the third-largest U.S. pharmacy chain at about 1,700 stores, primarily in
the Northeast and West Coast. Rite Aid codes cleanly as a drugstore on every card we
track, so any drugstore-bonus card will earn here. The chain's BonusCash and wellness+
rewards programs layer on top of card cashback.

stores.name: Ross Dress for Less
stores.intro:
Ross Dress for Less is a U.S. off-price retail chain with about 1,800 stores
selling apparel, accessories, and home goods at discount prices. Codes as
department stores on most cards. There's no co-branded Ross credit card, so the
best pairings are general department-store-bonus cards (Wells Fargo Active Cash
flat 2%, U.S. Bank Shopper Cash Rewards if Ross is selected) or flat-rate
cashback cards. Ross doesn't run a loyalty program.

stores.name: Safeway
stores.intro:
Safeway is one of Albertsons' largest banners, running about 900 stores primarily in the
Western U.S. Safeway codes as groceries at the register and most online ordering happens
through safeway.com or DoorDash partnerships. Safeway's own Just for U digital coupons
stack with whatever card-side cashback you earn at checkout.

stores.name: Saks Fifth Avenue
stores.intro:
Saks Fifth Avenue is a high-end department store with about 40 full-line stores plus the
much larger Saks Off 5th outlet network. Average ticket sizes are higher than at
mass-market department chains, which makes a high-rate department-store category card
more valuable here than at, say, JCPenney. Saks codes as a department store in-store and
online shopping on saksfifthavenue.com, so general-purpose category bonuses apply.

stores.name: Sam's Club
stores.intro:
Sam's Club is Walmart's warehouse-club arm, with about 600 U.S. clubs and a steadily
growing online and Scan & Go catalog. Member tiers (Club vs. Plus) affect free-shipping
and curbside perks but not the rewards math at the register. Sam's accepts Visa,
Mastercard, Amex, and Discover, and Sam's Club gas pumps code as wholesale clubs rather
than gas stations — the same rule as Costco.

stores.name: Sephora
stores.intro:
Sephora is the largest U.S. beauty specialty retailer at about 600 stores, plus a
separate shop-in-shop presence inside Kohl's. Beauty doesn't have its own reward
category on most general-purpose cards, so in-store purchases at Sephora typically fall
back to a flat-rate cashback card. Online purchases at sephora.com code as online
shopping. Sephora's own Beauty Insider points layer on top of card cashback.

stores.name: Shake Shack
stores.intro:
Shake Shack is a U.S. fast-casual burger chain with about 350 domestic locations,
with higher average ticket sizes than traditional fast-food. Charges code as
dining on every card we track, so any dining-bonus card applies (Amex Gold 4x,
Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%). Shake Shack runs a Shack Track loyalty
program in its app with progressive item rewards stackable with card bonuses.

stores.name: Sheetz
stores.intro:
Sheetz is a Mid-Atlantic gas-and-convenience chain known for its made-to-order food
("MTO" subs, sides, smoothies). Fuel codes as gas on every card we track; in-store
food typically codes as gas or convenience and only rarely as dining. The free
Sheetz Rewardz app stacks per-gallon discounts and free food rewards on top of any
card's bonus rate, which usually beats hunting for a Sheetz-specific credit card.

stores.name: Shell
stores.intro:
Shell is one of the largest U.S. gasoline retailers by station count (about 13,000
locations), with a Fuel Rewards loyalty program tied to the Shell app. Shell pumps code
cleanly as gas on every card we track. The Shell Fuel Rewards program offers stackable
per-gallon discounts that are calculated separately from card cashback.

stores.name: Shipt
stores.intro:
Shipt is a same-day delivery service owned by Target, with grocery, household, and
some specialty retail (Petco, CVS) partners. Most Shipt charges code as groceries
or as the underlying merchant — worth checking your statement before relying on
a category-bonus card. Shipt is bundled into Target Circle 360 (Target's premium
membership), so heavy Target shoppers often pair it with a Target REDcard for
the in-store discount.

stores.name: ShopRite
stores.intro:
ShopRite is a cooperative grocery chain with about 280 stores across the Northeast
and Mid-Atlantic. Codes cleanly as groceries on every card we track. Strongest
pairings are Blue Cash Preferred (6%), Capital One SavorOne (3%), and U.S. Bank
Shopper Cash Rewards (6% if ShopRite is one of the two selected retailers). The
Price Plus Club loyalty program runs digital coupons and per-gallon fuel savings
at participating partner stations on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Sonic Drive-In
stores.intro:
Sonic Drive-In is a U.S. drive-in fast-food chain with about 3,500 domestic
locations, primarily in the South and Midwest. Charges code as dining on every
card we track. Any dining-bonus card applies (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x,
SavorOne 3%). The Sonic app's My SONIC Rewards program issues per-purchase points
and limited-time half-price drink and burger promotions that stack on top of card
bonuses.

stores.name: Southwest Airlines
stores.intro:
Southwest Airlines is the largest U.S. domestic carrier by passenger volume and the
home of the Rapid Rewards program. Tickets code as airfare on every card we track,
and the Companion Pass — earned by hitting an annual points threshold — is widely
considered the best perk in U.S. domestic travel. Co-branded Chase Rapid Rewards
cards count signup bonuses toward Companion Pass qualification, which is the main
reason most travelers carry one. Southwest doesn't sell tickets through OTAs.

stores.name: Speedway
stores.intro:
Speedway is a Midwest-and-East gas-and-convenience chain operated by 7-Eleven
parent 7-Eleven, Inc. Pumps code as gas on every card we track. The Speedy Rewards
program is unusually generous on convenience-store snacks and drinks, often more
valuable per visit than the gas-card rebate. Heavy Speedway customers pair a 4-5%
general gas card with the Speedy Rewards app rather than chase a co-brand option.

stores.name: Spirit Airlines
stores.intro:
Spirit Airlines is a U.S. ultra-low-cost carrier known for stripped-down base fares
with à la carte pricing for bags, seat selection, and refreshments. Tickets code as
airfare on every card we track, but for cards that offer trip-delay or trip-cancel
insurance the small base fare often falls below useful coverage thresholds. There's
no widely-issued co-branded Spirit credit card, so general travel cards (Sapphire
Preferred, Venture, Capital One Savor) are typically the best pairing.

stores.name: Spotify
stores.intro:
Spotify is the largest U.S. music-streaming service. Monthly Spotify subscriptions
code as streaming on every card we track. Streaming-bonus cards (Blue Cash
Preferred 6%, U.S. Bank Cash+ 5% on selected categories) are the strongest
pairings. The American Express Platinum offers digital-entertainment statement
credits that occasionally apply to Spotify; check current terms before relying
on it.

stores.name: Sprouts Farmers Market
stores.intro:
Sprouts Farmers Market is a natural-and-organic grocery chain with about 400 U.S.
stores, leaning heavily on fresh produce and bulk foods. Sprouts codes cleanly as
groceries on every card we track. The strongest pairings are 6% Blue Cash Preferred
and the U.S. Bank Shopper Cash Rewards (if Sprouts is selected as a chosen retailer);
Capital One SavorOne earns 3% on groceries with no cap. Sprouts doesn't run a
co-branded card, so general grocery cards are the standard answer.

stores.name: Staples
stores.intro:
Staples is a U.S. office-supply retailer with about 1,000 stores plus a strong
business-to-business operation. In-store and online charges code as office
supplies on every card we track. The Chase Ink Business Cash earns 5% back at
office-supply stores including Staples (capped at $25K/yr in combined office
supply + telecom spend) — typically the strongest pairing for small-business
cardholders. Staples also runs a Rewards program issuing points-back on
recycling and ink purchases.

stores.name: Starbucks
stores.intro:
Starbucks runs about 16,500 U.S. stores plus the Starbucks app, which is itself one of
the largest closed-loop payment systems in the country. Whether you pay through the app,
with a physical Starbucks card, or directly with a credit card, the underlying Starbucks
transaction codes as dining/restaurants on every card we track. Reloading your Starbucks
balance via card is the cleanest way to capture the dining bonus on cards that pay 3-5%
on dining.

stores.name: Stop & Shop
stores.intro:
Stop & Shop is a Northeast U.S. grocery chain with about 400 stores, also owned by
Ahold Delhaize. Codes cleanly as groceries on every card we track. Best pairings
are Blue Cash Preferred (6%), Capital One SavorOne (3%), and U.S. Bank Shopper Cash
Rewards (6% on selected retailers including most major grocers). The Go Rewards
loyalty program runs separate per-gallon discounts at Stop & Shop fuel stations
that stack on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Subway
stores.intro:
Subway is the largest restaurant chain in the U.S. by store count, with about
20,000 domestic locations. Charges code as dining on every card we track. Standard
high-bonus dining cards (Amex Gold 4x capped, Sapphire Preferred 3x, Capital One
SavorOne 3%) are the obvious pairings. The MyWay Rewards loyalty program and
recurring digital coupons in the Subway app stack on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Sunoco
stores.intro:
Sunoco-branded stations cover about 5,200 U.S. locations primarily across the East
and Midwest. Pumps code cleanly as gas on every card we track. The Sunoco Rewards
app stacks 5-10 cents per gallon savings on top of any card's bonus rate. There's
a Sunoco Rewards co-branded credit card, but in absolute cashback most 4-5%
general gas cards beat it.

stores.name: Taco Bell
stores.intro:
Taco Bell is a U.S. fast-food chain with about 7,500 domestic locations, owned by
Yum! Brands. Charges code as dining on every card we track. Standard dining cards
(Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%) earn the bonus. The Taco Bell
Rewards app issues frequent free-item promotions and limited-time member-exclusive
menu items that stack on top of any card's bonus rate.

stores.name: Target

also_earns.note: If selected as one of your 2 quarterly retailers (up to $1,500/quarter, then 1.5%)

stores.intro:
Target sits in the same awkward bucket as Walmart for general-purpose credit cards: it
codes as a discount store rather than a supermarket or department store, so most cards'
bonus categories (grocery, dining, department stores) miss it entirely. Target.com
purchases code as online shopping on most cards, which is where the highest
non-co-branded earn rates show up. In-store, you're typically falling back to a strong
flat-rate card unless you carry a Target-issued card for the 5% RedCard discount applied
at checkout.

stores.name: Texas Roadhouse
stores.intro:
Texas Roadhouse is a U.S. casual-dining steakhouse chain with about 700 domestic
locations. Charges code as dining on every card we track. Any dining-bonus card
earns the bonus (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%). The chain
doesn't run a high-volume loyalty program, so the card bonus is the main
rewards lever per visit.

stores.name: The Cheesecake Factory
stores.intro:
The Cheesecake Factory is a U.S. casual-dining chain with about 220 domestic
locations, with notably higher average tickets than typical casual dining.
Charges code as dining on every card we track, so any dining-bonus card applies
(Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%). The chain runs Cheesecake
Rewards in its app, with free slices on birthdays and milestone visits.

stores.name: The North Face
stores.intro:
The North Face is a U.S. outdoor-apparel brand owned by VF Corporation, with
about 150 retail stores plus extensive third-party distribution at REI, Dick's,
Backcountry, and more. Direct purchases at thenorthface.com code as online
shopping; in-store charges code as department stores or apparel. The XPLR Pass
loyalty program is free and issues spend rewards plus first-access to limited
drops. Pair with an online-shopping-bonus card or flat-rate cashback.

stores.name: TJ Maxx
stores.intro:
TJ Maxx is the flagship off-price retail brand of TJX Companies, with about 1,300
U.S. stores selling apparel, home goods, and accessories at discount prices.
Codes as department stores on most cards, so a department-store-bonus card
(Sapphire Preferred 3x on online department stores via portal, U.S. Bank Shopper
Cash Rewards if selected) is the strongest pairing. The TJX Rewards co-branded
Mastercard offers 5% back at TJ Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods, Sierra, and
Homesense — typically the best rate for heavy TJX shoppers.

stores.name: Trader Joe's
stores.intro:
Trader Joe's is the smaller-format private-label grocer with about 600 stores
nationwide, famous for store-brand goods and tight pricing. Average tickets are smaller
than at full-line supermarkets, but the visit frequency tends to be higher. TJ's codes
cleanly as groceries (supermarket merchant code) on essentially every card we track, so
any standard 3-6% grocery card will earn here.

stores.name: Uber
stores.intro:
Uber is the largest U.S. rideshare service. Uber rides code under the transit
category on most cards (and as ground transportation on a few). The Amex Platinum
and Amex Gold include monthly Uber Cash credits that apply to both rides and Uber
Eats orders. Several cards (Capital One Venture X, Sapphire Reserve) earn elevated
rates on rideshare via their travel multipliers. Uber One membership stacks with
some card credits but is rarely earned back unless you ride or order frequently.

stores.name: Uber Eats
stores.intro:
Uber Eats is the second-largest U.S. food-delivery service and is bundled with Uber
rideshare for many users. Restaurant orders code as dining/restaurants on every card we
track, and several cards (notably the Amex Gold and Platinum) include Uber Cash credits
that can be applied to Uber Eats orders. Grocery orders through Uber Eats typically code
as the underlying merchant.

stores.name: Ulta Beauty
stores.intro:
Ulta Beauty is the largest U.S. beauty retailer by store count at about 1,400 locations,
covering both prestige and mass-market lines under one roof. Like Sephora, Ulta in-store
purchases don't trigger a dedicated card category, so the right card there is usually a
strong flat-rate. Online purchases at ulta.com code as online shopping. Ultamate Rewards
loyalty points stack with card cashback.

stores.name: Uniqlo
stores.intro:
Uniqlo is a Japan-headquartered apparel chain with about 65 U.S. stores, focused
on basics and technical fabrics like Heattech and AIRism. In-store charges code
as department stores or apparel; uniqlo.com codes as online shopping. There's
no co-branded U.S. credit card, so the strongest pairings are an online-shopping
card for uniqlo.com or a flat-rate cashback card in store.

stores.name: United Airlines
stores.intro:
United Airlines is one of the three U.S. legacy carriers and runs the MileagePlus
loyalty program. United tickets code as airfare on every card we track. The Chase
co-branded ladder ranges from no-fee Gateway to the $525 Club Infinite with United
Club lounge membership; mid-tier Explorer adds free checked bag and 2 club passes,
and Quest adds an annual flight credit. General travel cards often outperform on
cash-equivalent value, but United co-brands waive close-in award fees and add
status-credit boosts that frequent flyers value.

stores.name: Victoria's Secret
stores.intro:
Victoria's Secret is a U.S. lingerie and beauty retailer with about 800 domestic
stores in its main brand plus the PINK youth-focused sister brand. In-store
charges code as department stores; victoriassecret.com codes as online shopping.
The Victoria's Secret Credit Card and Victoria's Secret Mastercard offer tiered
cashback at VS, PINK, and Bath & Body Works (sister brands). The Love Rewards
loyalty program is free and stacks with card cashback.

stores.name: Vrbo
stores.intro:
Vrbo is the second-largest U.S. vacation-rental marketplace after Airbnb, owned
by Expedia Group. Bookings code as travel on every card we track and as hotels
on a few. Unlike Airbnb, Vrbo focuses exclusively on whole-home rentals (no
shared spaces). Vrbo bookings count for the Sapphire Reserve and Venture X
travel-credit categories. Booking through a card's travel portal earns the
portal multiplier but typically loses Vrbo Boost host-program perks.

stores.name: Walgreens
stores.intro:
Walgreens is the second-largest U.S. pharmacy chain at about 8,500 stores, with a big
convenience-goods footprint alongside the pharmacy business. Walgreens codes as a
drugstore and is one of the merchants that earns 3% on the Apple Card via Apple Pay, in
addition to general drugstore-category bonuses on other cards.

stores.name: Walmart

also_earns.note: If selected as one of your 2 quarterly retailers (up to $1,500/quarter, then 1.5%)

stores.intro:
Walmart is the country's largest grocer and one of its largest general retailers, but it
sits in an awkward spot for credit-card rewards. Most cards' grocery 3-5% bonuses
explicitly exclude Walmart (it codes as a discount store, not a supermarket), and
Walmart's gas stations code as Walmart, not gas. Walmart.com purchases code as online
shopping on most cards, which opens up the strongest non-co-branded earn rates —
in-store purchases generally fall back to flat-rate cashback unless you're using a
Walmart-issued card.

stores.name: Wawa
stores.intro:
Wawa is a regional gas-and-convenience chain in the Mid-Atlantic and Florida with
a cult following for its fresh hoagies and built-to-order food. Fuel codes as
gas on every card we track; in-store food can code as gas, convenience, or in
some cases dining depending on the bank's classification. There's no widely-issued
Wawa-specific credit card, so general 4-5% gas cards plus the free Wawa Rewards
app are the standard pairing.

stores.name: Wayfair
stores.intro:
Wayfair is the largest U.S. online-only home goods retailer, plus a few flagship
physical stores under sister brands AllModern and Joss & Main. All purchases
code as online shopping on every card we track; some banks classify them as
furniture. The Wayfair Mastercard offers 5% rewards in Wayfair Rewards points
on Wayfair-family purchases — typically the strongest rate for heavy buyers.
Otherwise pair with an online-shopping-bonus card.

stores.name: Wegmans
stores.intro:
Wegmans is a regional supermarket chain with about 110 stores in the Northeast,
well-known for prepared foods, in-store dining, and a deep wine and beer selection.
Pricing skews mid-to-high and average tickets are larger than at typical mass-market
supermarkets. Wegmans codes as groceries on essentially every card we track.

stores.name: Wendy's
stores.intro:
Wendy's is a U.S. fast-food chain with about 6,000 domestic locations, known for
fresh-never-frozen beef and the Frosty. Charges code as dining on every card we
track, so Amex Gold 4x (within $50K cap), Sapphire Preferred 3x, and SavorOne 3%
all earn the dining bonus. The Wendy's Rewards program in the app issues frequent
free-item offers that stack on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Whole Foods Market
stores.intro:
Whole Foods Market is Amazon's premium grocery arm, with about 530 U.S. stores. Pricing
is higher than at mass-market supermarkets, which makes a strong grocery-category card
more valuable per visit. The standout here is the Amazon Prime Rewards Visa: it earns 5%
at Whole Foods (with a Prime membership), which is the strongest non-promotional grocery
rate available. Without Prime, fall back to whichever 3-6% supermarket card you carry.

stores.name: Wyndham
stores.intro:
Wyndham Hotels & Resorts is the largest U.S. hotel chain by property count — about
9,000 hotels — concentrated in mid-scale and economy brands like Days Inn, Super 8,
La Quinta, and Ramada, with a smaller upscale presence under Wyndham Grand and the
Registry Collection. Wyndham Rewards uses a flat or tiered award chart with redemptions
starting at 7,500 points per night, and the program partners with Vacasa for full
vacation-rental redemptions — a quirk that gives Wyndham points unusual reach for an
economy-leaning chain. Direct bookings at wyndhamhotels.com or in the app earn points;
OTA stays do not. Co-brand cards run lower annual fees than the major chains' cards,
matching the program's value-tier identity.
